message:
fix(scan_pipeline): use definitive batch scan at flush for exact trigger parity

The chunked trigger scanning used an overlap-and-filter approach to detect
patterns split across chunk boundaries. However, Aho-Corasick LeftmostFirst
non-overlapping matching is context-dependent: matches earlier in the stream
can "consume" bytes that prevent later matches, and this consumption context
is lost when re-scanning the overlap buffer. This caused the proptest
chunked_batch_trigger_parity_arbitrary_boundaries to fail.

Fix: accumulate raw chunk data in a trigger_data_buffer. At flush time,
perform a definitive batch scan on the full accumulated buffer, giving
exact parity with process(). The incremental overlap-based scan is kept
for real-time feedback (has_errors(), has_completions()).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
